title: Paul Thomas Anderson Wins Best Director for 'One Battle After Another' at 2026 Academy Awards
text: Paul Thomas Anderson claimed the Best Director Oscar at the 2026 Academy Awards for his film "One Battle After Another." This marks a significant milestone for the acclaimed filmmaker—his first-ever Academy Award for directing, despite four prior nominations in the category. The victory came on a successful night for the film overall. Earlier in the ceremony, "One Battle After Another" secured Best Adapted Screenplay, which Anderson won as well. This second Oscar of the evening reflected the film's strong performance at the ceremony. Anderson competed against several notable directors, including Ryan Coogler (nominated for the vampire horror film "Sinners"), Chloé Zhao ("Hamnet"), Josh Safdie ("Marty Supreme"), and Joachim Trier ("Sentimental Value"). The recognition represents a career achievement for Anderson, marking the legendary filmmaker's first-ever Academy Award for Best Director, and his second Oscar ever after winning Best Adapted Screenplay earlier in the night.
